$comment: D-CSUBSET-VLA C5: the runtime witness that block-scope VLA teardown reclaims SP on every non-return exit. A VLA in a 400000-iteration loop body (phase 1) would STATUS_STACK_OVERFLOW (0xC00000FD) without the per-iteration StackRestore — this example is a REAL crash-on-disable, not just a byte pin. It also exercises the continue / break / nested-block / goto-out exit edges and checks a deterministic running total (1200012), so a mis-PLACED restore flips the exit code too. The `release` arm proves the optimizer preserves the save/restore over the new MIR shapes (StackSave/StackRestore are side-effecting + memory-clobbering, never DCE'd/reordered). exitCode 42 iff every VLA scope was torn down AND every value survived.
